name: Oven Baked Bar-b-q Brisket
recipeInstructions:
1. OVERNIGHT PREPARATION: In a small bowl, combine the seasoning ingredients. Rub the brisket all over with the seasoning blend.
2. Place a large sheet of heavy-duty aluminum foil in a 9 x 13 pan. Place the brisket in the pan and add the liquid smoke and worcestershire sauce. Fold the foil securely around the brisket to seal it.
3. Place the pan in the refrigerator to marinate overnight.
4. TO BAKE: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit. Remove the pan from the refrigerator and bake the brisket for about 4 1/2 hours.
5. Unseal the foil and add 1 cup of Head Country Bar-B-Q Sauce over the brisket. Cook uncovered for an additional 45 minutes to one hour.
6. TO SERVE: Remove the brisket from the pan and let it rest on a cutting surface for 15 to 20 minutes. Slice across the meat's grain and serve. Reserve the sauce from the pan and thicken if desired, or serve as is on the side.
7. TO MAKE CHOPPED BAR-B-Q BEEF: Chop the cooked brisket. Place in a large sauté pan and add 1 cup of Head Country Bar-B-Q Sauce. Cook over medium heat, stirring frequently, until thickened. Serve on toasted buns for a delicious sandwich.
